The long-term plan for this project. CLAUDE.md is the authoritative
source for active rules; ROADMAP is for sequencing.

## Tracks

Narrative phase entries live in `roadmap/<track>.md` so parallel
Claude Code sessions can edit different tracks without merge
conflicts. Update the relevant track file in the same commit as the
work that lands.

- [Analytical / agent phases](./roadmap/analytical.md) — Phases 1–7
  (toolkit, freshness, neighborhood, velocity, spatial context,
  statistical refinement, visual layer, reasoning agent).
- [UI](./roadmap/ui.md) — frontend pages, components, estimation
  flow.
- [Map](./roadmap/map.md) — typed locality IDs, admin boundaries.
- [Scraper](./roadmap/scraper.md) — ingestion, multi-portal,
  category coverage.
- [Operator workflow](./roadmap/operator-workflow.md) — curation,
  collections, tags, notes.
- [Summarize](./roadmap/summarize.md) — LLM-derived natural-language
  annotations.

## Out of scope until explicitly opened
- ClickUp integration.
- MCP server wrapping the toolkit (for ad-hoc chat with the data).
- Public read API beyond the bearer-token gate.
- Per-user identity / accounts in the UI (the `anon` key is shared and
  read-only; the FastAPI token is shared and gated).

## Data preconditions
- Velocity tools (Phase 3b) work today (1 snapshot per listing is enough
  for TOM math).
- Outlier history-pattern detection (Phase 3a) becomes more useful as
  snapshot density grows past ~1.5/listing average.
- Cluster detection (Phase 5) needs neighborhoods with 30+ comparables
  to be meaningful; sparse rural areas will return single-cluster
  results.
